# synix

> A build system for agent memory

## What it does

Synix transforms raw conversations into searchable, hierarchical memory with full provenance tracking. Declarative pipelines define how conversation exports become episodes, summaries, rollups, and core memory. When you change a config, only affected layers rebuild. The output is system prompt + RAG built from conversations with complete lineage.

## Key concepts

- **Artifact** — immutable, versioned build output (transcript, episode, rollup, core memory) with content-addressed SHA256 identity
- **Layer** — typed processing step in the build DAG. Source for inputs, Transform subclasses for LLM steps, SearchSurface/SynixSearch/FlatFile for projections
- **Pipeline** — declared in Python using Pipeline.add(*layers). Dependencies via object references, not strings
- **Projection** — structured output declaration materialized at release time (search.db, context.md)
- **Release** — synix release HEAD --to <name> materializes projections from snapshot to .synix/releases/<name>/
- **Provenance** — every artifact traces to inputs via parent_labels. Always included in search results
- **Cache/Rebuild** — fingerprint comparison: if inputs or transform changed, rebuild. Otherwise skip

## Installation and quick start

```bash
# Install
uvx synix init my-project
cd my-project

# Add API key (see pipeline.py), then build and release
uvx synix build                   # produce immutable snapshot
uvx synix release HEAD --to local # materialize search.db, context.md

# Use
uvx synix search "hiking" --release local
uvx synix list                    # all artifacts
uvx synix show final-report       # render an artifact
uvx synix lineage <artifact-id>   # full provenance chain
```

## CLI commands

- `synix build pipeline.py` — execute pipeline, produce snapshot in .synix/
- `synix plan pipeline.py` — dry-run showing rebuild/cached decisions
- `synix release HEAD --to local` — materialize projections to named release
- `synix search "query" --release local` — query materialized release
- `synix list [--ref HEAD]` — artifacts in snapshot
- `synix show <label> [--ref HEAD]` — display artifact content
- `synix lineage <artifact-id>` — provenance tree from .synix/
- `synix diff <ref-a> <ref-b>` — structured comparison
- `synix clean` — remove .synix/releases/ and .synix/work/
- `synix init <name>` — scaffold new project

## Architecture overview

```
src/synix/
├── core/models.py          # Layer hierarchy, Pipeline, Transform base classes
├── build/
│   ├── runner.py          # Execute pipeline, walk DAG, run transforms, cache artifacts
│   ├── snapshot_view.py   # Read API over .synix/ refs and objects
│   ├── release_engine.py  # Materialize projections via adapters
│   ├── adapters.py        # Projection adapter contract (plan/apply/verify)
│   └── llm_transforms.py  # Built-in memory transforms
├── search/
│   ├── indexer.py         # SQLite FTS5 + embedding indexing
│   ├── retriever.py       # Hybrid search with RRF fusion
│   └── adapter.py         # SynixSearch projection adapter
├── ext/                   # Generic platform transforms
│   ├── map_synthesis.py   # 1:1 MapSynthesis transform
│   ├── group_synthesis.py # N:M GroupSynthesis transform
│   └── reduce_synthesis.py# N:1 ReduceSynthesis transform
├── adapters/              # Source file parsers
│   ├── chatgpt.py        # ChatGPT JSON exports
│   ├── claude.py         # Claude JSON exports
│   └── text.py           # Text/markdown with YAML frontmatter
└── cli/                  # Click CLI commands
```

## Pipeline definition

Pipeline definitions are Python code with object references:

```python
from synix import Pipeline, Source, SearchSurface, SynixSearch
from synix.ext import EpisodeSummary, MonthlyRollup, CoreSynthesis

pipeline = Pipeline("my-pipeline")
pipeline.source_dir = "./sources"
pipeline.llm_config = {"provider": "anthropic", "model": "claude-haiku"}

# Define layers
transcripts = Source("transcripts")
episodes = EpisodeSummary("episodes", depends_on=[transcripts])
monthly = MonthlyRollup("monthly", depends_on=[episodes])
core = CoreSynthesis("core", depends_on=[monthly])

# Define search surface and output
memory_search = SearchSurface(
    "memory-search", 
    sources=[episodes, monthly, core], 
    modes=["fulltext", "semantic"]
)

# Add to pipeline
pipeline.add(transcripts, episodes, monthly, core, memory_search)
pipeline.add(SynixSearch("search", surface=memory_search))
```

Transform types: MapSynthesis (1:1), GroupSynthesis (N:M), ReduceSynthesis (N:1), FoldSynthesis (N:1 sequential)

## Important constraints

- SQLite only — no external databases required
- No web UI — CLI and Python API only
- Build separates from release — synix build produces snapshots, synix release materializes outputs
- Single source of truth in .synix/ — no build/ directory
- Providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, or OpenAI-compatible via base_url
- Python 3.11+ required
